articles.title: GET YOUR BRAND IN FRONT OF 4+ MILLION TECH PROFESSIONALS
articles.summary: If you're looking to get in front of a highly engaged tech audience including software devs, AI/ML engineers and AI enthusiasts, consider advertising in TLDR. We have 10 newsletters to choose from and a subscriber base of 4+ million tech professionals to effectively reach your target audience. Learn more [4] about running your first campaign with us.

articles.title: A HACKER STOLE OPENAI SECRETS, RAISING FEARS THAT CHINA COULD, TOO
articles.summary: A hacker gained access to OpenAI's internal messaging systems early last year. They accessed details from discussions in an internal online forum but did not get into the systems where the company houses and builds its artificial intelligence. Details of the incident were revealed to employees in April 2023. Executives decided not to share the news publicly because no information about customers or partners was stolen. It is believed that the hacker was a private individual with no known ties to a foreign government. OpenAI did not inform the FBI or anyone else in law enforcement about the incident.

articles.title: FIGMA PULLS AI TOOL AFTER CRITICISM THAT IT RIPPED OFF APPLE'S DESIGN
articles.summary: Figma's "Make Designs" AI feature was temporarily pulled amidst concerns it generated designs too similar to Apple's apps. CEO Dylan Field attributes the similarity to third-party models and a design system the company commissioned, rather than training on Figma's own content. Figma plans to ensure future AI features align with quality standards and user privacy choices.

articles.title: AI RESURRECTS DECEASED ACTORS' VOICES TO READ AUDIOBOOKS
articles.summary: AI company ElevenLabs has launched a Reader app featuring voice-overs by digital versions of deceased celebrities, stirring questions of morality and legality in AI's use in media. While partnerships with estates enable monetization, concerns about authenticity and ethical implications persist. The emergence of such technologies heralds a potential expansion in markets such as audiobooks, where celebrity voices drive demand.

articles.title: OPTIMIZING SUB BILLION PARAMETER MODELS
articles.summary: Thorough and interesting paper by Meta that shows how to train small models to be as performant as possible.

articles.title: CAPPA TRAINING REPORT
articles.summary: The CapPa vision model trained by Craiyon shows state-of-the-art performance on a number of challenging vision benchmarks.

articles.title: MEDICAL IMAGING
articles.summary: Researchers have developed a new generative model to improve MRI image translation between different sequences without requiring paired samples.

articles.title: SDXL TRAINED FROM SCRATCH WITH A FULL LLM EMBEDDER (HUGGING FACE HUB)
articles.summary: A great and open alternative diffusion model with strong text understanding.

articles.title: POINT CLOUD COMPLETION
articles.summary: EGIInet introduces a new approach to point cloud completion by effectively combining two modalities through geometric task guidance.

articles.title: REAL-TIME 3D RECONSTRUCTION FOR SURGERY
articles.summary: An SfM-free method for 3D reconstruction of surgical scenes has been introduced, addressing the limitations of previous approaches that struggled with minimal textures and photometric inconsistencies.

articles.title: FUN AUDIO GENERATION AND UNDERSTANDING
articles.summary: The Tongyi speech team released extremely strong audio understanding and generation models.

articles.title: SUPERINTELLIGENCE—10 YEARS LATER
articles.summary: A decade after Nick Bostrom's influential book "Superintelligence," AI advancements have brought the possibility of AGI and its risks into mainstream discussions. The AI research community is now urgently addressing AI safety, with 2024 marking a pivot toward ensuring control and alignment with human values. The field grapples with ethical and safety challenges previously considered speculative as AI technologies rapidly progress.

articles.title: GOOGLE SMART PASTE
articles.summary: Google's core code tooling team has trained a smart paste model that adjusts code pasted in based on the context. They found a 42% acceptance rate for 6% of all copied code.

articles.title: QUALITY PROMPTS
articles.summary: This library consolidates a growing set of standard prompting tricks into a single place and can automatically apply them to your prompts to optimize them.

articles.title: APPLE M5 CHIP'S DUAL-USE DESIGN WILL POWER FUTURE MACS AND AI SERVERS
articles.summary: Apple will reportedly use a more advanced System on Integrated Chip (SoIC) technology for its M5 chips.

articles.title: APPLE INTELLIGENCE AND A BETTER SIRI MAY BE COMING TO IPHONES THIS SPRING
articles.summary: Apple's Siri is set to improve with the iOS 18.4 update releasing in Spring 2025, which will introduce new features like advanced photo searching and possibly AI-driven emoji and writing tools.
